chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.945 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani. Abdul Rauf..............Vs........................ The State 13.05.2026. Mr. Insaf Ahmned, Advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Addl. P.G. a/w Insp. Syed Farman Ali Shah, P.S. Gulshan-e-Iqbal, Karachi. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested on 23.11.2025 from Jamali Goth via Chatta Gabol Goth, Malir, Karachi by a police team of P.S. Sohrab Goth and from him allegedly 970 grams of Ice was recovered, hence he was booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.758/2025 U/s 6,9 5, Sindh CNS Amended Act, 2025 of P.S. Sohrab Goth, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel for applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that no video recording of the incident is made and the applicant’s career is spotless as no other case is registered against him. 3. On the other hand, learned Addl. P.G. has opposed the bail. I.O. is present and submits that video recording of the incident has been done however, he admits that nowhere in police papers this fact is mentioned and even the trial court has confirmed this fact in the impugned order. It appears that due to inefficiency and negligence of the I.O, prima facie, the prosecution case has become weak as important compliance u/s 17 of Sindh CNS Act, although claimed to have been done, has not been mentioned in the Challan or in any other document making the case against the applicant to be one of further inquiry, and which makes the applicant entitled to grant of bail. That prima facie is further reinforced from the fact that applicant is the first offender.

It appears that due to inefficiency and negligence of the I.O, prima facie, the prosecution case has become weak as important compliance u/s 17 of Sindh CNS Act, although claimed to have been done, has not been mentioned in the Challan or in any other document making the case against the applicant to be one of further inquiry, and which makes the applicant entitled to grant of bail. That prima facie is further reinforced from the fact that applicant is the first offender. Hence this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
